abstract.t: This document defines the Signed Email Authentication Layer (SEAL), a cryptographically signed identity envelope carried within a new message header field, SEAL-Envelope. SEAL provides a stable, forwarding-resilient identity assertion that is independent of the mutable RFC 5322 header block and message body. SEAL is designed to complement or replace DKIM and ARC by eliminating their dependency on mutable message components and by providing a canonical, tamper- evident identity layer that survives transit through intermediaries.
